concerns.text: The synthetic validation dataset (steps 8‑10 in the Methodology sketch) generates ground‑truth p‑values using the same SciPy implementation that the audit pipeline later uses for reconstruction. Because the ground truth and the system under test rely on identical code paths, any systematic bias or bug in the statistical computation will be shared, violating the independence required for a valid validation. Use an alternative calculation method (e.g., analytical formulas or a different library) to generate the true p‑values, or cross‑validate with hand‑computed cases.

FR‑004 flags a summary as inconsistent when the absolute difference between reported and reconstructed p‑values exceeds 0.05. Absolute p‑value differences are not scale‑invariant; a 0.05 gap is huge for p = 0.001 but negligible for p = 0.8. This threshold can produce many false positives/negatives and is not a standard statistical practice. Consider using a relative difference, log‑scale comparison, or a significance test (e.g., comparing the reported test statistic to the reconstructed one) instead of a fixed 0.05 absolute cutoff.
